title: Epidanthus goniorhachis
taxonomy: Epidanthus goniorhachis is classified under the family Gesneriaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint, sage, and honeysuckle. The order Lamiales is a subgroup of the clade Asterids, a large and diverse clade of flowering plants. Within the Asterids, the Lamiales order is part of the larger clade of core eudicots, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of roses, carrots, and sunflowers. The classification of Epidanthus goniorhachis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Epidanthus goniorhachis. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on the medicinal uses of Epidanthus goniorhachis.
